(\n21\t\t\"errors\"\n22\t\t\"fmt\"\n23\t\t\"os\"\n24\t\t\"strings\"\n25\t)\n26\t\n27\t// YAMLError is the base interface for all YAML processing errors.\n28\t//\n29\t// This interface provides a common type for all errors that occur during\n30\t// YAML file I/O, parsing, validation, and schema operations.\n31\ttype YAMLError interface {\n32\t\terror\n33\t\n34\t\t// Code returns the error code for programmatic error handling.\n35\t\tCode() ErrorCode\n36\t\n37\t\t// YAMLErrorType returns the category of error for type switching.\n38\t\tYAMLErrorType() ErrorType\n39\t\n40\t\t// Context (\n48\t\tErrorTypeFile           ErrorType = \"file\"           // File I/O errors\n49\t\tErrorTypeParse          ErrorType = \"parse\"          // YAML parsing errors\n50\t\tErrorTypeSyntax         ErrorType = \"syntax\"         // YAML syntax errors\n51\t\tErrorTypeStructure      ErrorType = \"structure\"      // YAML structure errors\n52\t\tErrorTypeTypeMismatch   ErrorType = \"type_mismatch\"  // Type conversion errors\n53\t\tErrorTypeValidation     ErrorType = \"validation\"     // Validation errors\n54\t\tErrorTypeFieldNotFound  ErrorType = \"field_not_found\" // Missing required fields\n55\t\tErrorTypeConstraint     ErrorType = \"constraint\"     // Constraint violations\n56\t\tErrorTypeDuplicateKey   ErrorType = \"duplicate_key\"  // Duplicate key errors\n59\t\tErrorTypeSchemaValidate ErrorType = \"schema_validate\" // Schema validation errors\n60\t\tErrorTypeUnknown        ErrorType = \"unknown\"        // Unknown error type\n61\t\tErrorTypeEmpty          ErrorType = \"empty\"          // Empty content\n62\t\tErrorTypeIO             ErrorType = \"io\"             // I/O error\n63\t)\n64\t\n65\t// ParseErrorVariant represents the specific variant/category of a parse error.\n66\t//\n67\t// ParseErrorVariant provides a structured way to categorize different types of\n68\t// parse errors, enabling precise error handling and user-friendly error messages.\n69\t// Each variant represents a distinct category of parsing failure.\n70\ttype ParseErrorVariant string\n71\t\n72\tconst (\n73\t\t// ParseErrorVariantSyntax represents YAML syntax errors.\n74\t\t//\n75\t\t// This variant covers fundamental YAML syntax issues including:\n76\t\t// - Invalid indentation\n77\t\t// - Malformed YAML structure\n78\t\t// - Invalid escape sequences\n79\t\t// - Improper quoting\n80\t\t// - Invalid YAML tokens\n81\t\tParseErrorVariantSyntax ParseErrorVariant = \"syntax\"\n82\t\n83\t\t// ParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch represents type conversion errors.\n84\t\t//\n85\t\t// This variant covers errors when YAML content cannot be converted to the\n86\t\t// expected Go type, including:\n87\t\t// - Attempting to parse a string as an integer\n88\t\t// - Trying to unmarshal a scalar into a struct\n89\t\t// - Incompatible array/slice types\n90\t\t// - Invalid boolean representations\n91\t\tParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch ParseErrorVariant = \"type_mismatch\"\n92\t\n93\t\t// ParseErrorVariantValidation represents constraint validation errors.\n94\t\t//\n95\t\t// This variant covers errors when YAML data violates defined constraints,\n96\t\t// including:\n97\t\t// - Value out of allowed range\n98\t\t// - String length violations\n99\t\t// - Pattern matching failures\n100\t\t// - Required field violations\n101\t\tParseErrorVariantValidation ParseErrorVariant = \"validation\"\n102\t\n103\t\t// ParseErrorVariantIO represents I/O operation errors.\n104\t\t//\n105\t\t// This variant covers errors related to file operations during parsing,\n106\t\t// including:\n107\t\t// - File not found\n108\t\t// - Permission denied\n109\t\t// - Read/write failures\n110\t\t// - File system errors\n111\t\tParseErrorVariantIO ParseErrorVariant = \"io\"\n112\t\n113\t\t// ParseErrorVariantStructure represents YAML structure errors.\n114\t\t//\n115\t\t// This variant covers structural issues that syntax checking alone won't catch,\n116\t\t// including:\n117\t\t// - Duplicate mapping keys\n118\t\t// - Invalid nesting\n119\t\t// - Circular references\n120\t\t// - Inconsistent document structure\n121\t\tParseErrorVariantStructure ParseErrorVariant = \"structure\"\n122\t\n123\t\t// ParseErrorVariantCustom represents custom/extensible error categories.\n124\t\t//\n125\t\t// This variant is reserved for application-specific parsing errors that don't\n126\t\t// fit into the standard categories. It enables extensibility for domain-specific\n127\t\t// validation rules and custom parsing logic.\n128\t\t// Applications can use this variant with custom error codes and messages\n129\t\t// to represent specialized error conditions.\n130\t\tParseErrorVariantCustom ParseErrorVariant = \"custom\"\n131\t)\n132\t\n133\t// String returns the string representation of the ParseErrorVariant.\n134\tfunc (v ParseErrorVariant) String() string {\n135\t\treturn string(v)\n136\t}\n137\t\n138\t// Description returns a human-readable description of the ParseErrorVariant.\n139\tfunc (v ParseErrorVariant) Description() string {\n140\t\tswitch v {\n141\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantSyntax:\n142\t\t\treturn \"YAML syntax error - invalid YAML structure or formatting\"\n143\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch:\n144\t\t\treturn \"Type mismatch error - cannot convert YAML to expected type\"\n145\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantValidation:\n146\t\t\treturn \"Validation error - constraint violation or invalid value\"\n147\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantIO:\n148\t\t\treturn \"I/O error - file operation failed\"\n149\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantStructure:\n150\t\t\treturn \"Structure error - invalid YAML document structure\"\n151\t\tcase ParseErrorVariantCustom:\n152\t\t\treturn \"Custom error - application-specific parsing error\"\n153\t\tdefault:\n154\t\t\treturn \"Unknown parse error variant\"\n155\t\t}\n156\t}\n157\t\n158\t// ErrorCode represents specific error codes for programmatic error handling.\n159\t//\n160\t// ErrorCodes provide machine-readable identifiers for errors, enabling\n161\t// programmatic error handling and internationalization of error messages.\n162\ttype ErrorCode string\n163\t\n164\tconst (\n165\t\t// ============================================================================\n166\t\t// File Error Codes\n167\t\t// ============================================================================\n168\t\n169\t\t// ErrCodeFileNotFound indicates that a file does not exist at the specified path.\n170\t\t// This error is returned when file operations fail because the target file cannot\n171\t\t// be found in the filesystem.\n172\t\tErrCodeFileNotFound ErrorCode = \"FILE_NOT_FOUND\"\n173\t\n174\t\t// ErrCodeFileAccessDenied indicates insufficient permissions to access a file.\n175\t\t// This error occurs when the application lacks the necessary read or write\n176\t\t// permissions for the specified file path.\n177\t\tErrCodeFileAccessDenied ErrorCode = \"FILE_ACCESS_DENIED\"\n178\t\n179\t\t// ErrCodeFileIOError indicates a generic file I/O error that doesn't fit\n180\t\t// other specific file error categories. This includes disk errors, filesystem\n181\t\t// issues, or other low-level I/O failures.\n182\t\tErrCodeFileIOError ErrorCode = \"FILE_IO_ERROR\"\n183\t\n184\t\t// ErrCodeFileEmpty indicates that a file exists but contains no data or\n185\t\t// only whitespace. This error is used when parsing an empty file would result\n186\t\t// in undefined behavior or missing required configuration.\n187\t\tErrCodeFileEmpty ErrorCode = \"FILE_EMPTY\"\n188\t\n189\t\t// ============================================================================\n190\t\t// Parse Error Codes\n191\t\t// ============================================================================\n192\t\n193\t\t// ErrCodeInvalidSyntax indicates a YAML syntax error during parsing.\n194\t\t// This includes invalid indentation, malformed YAML structure, invalid escape\n195\t\t// sequences, improper quoting, or any fundamental YAML syntax violation that\n196\t\t// prevents the document from being parsed.\n197\t\tErrCodeInvalidSyntax ErrorCode = \"INVALID_SYNTAX\"\n198\t\n199\t\t// ErrCodeTypeMismatch indicates a type conversion error during parsing.\n200\t\t// This occurs when YAML content cannot be converted to the expected Go type,\n201\t\t// such as attempting to parse a string as an integer, unmarshaling a scalar into\n202\t\t// a struct, or incompatible array/slice types.\n203\t\tErrCodeTypeMismatch ErrorCode = \"TYPE_MISMATCH\"\n204\t\n205\t\t// ErrCodeInvalidStructure indicates a YAML structure error that passes\n206\t\t// syntax checking but represents invalid document structure. This includes\n207\t\t// invalid nesting, inconsistent document structure, or other structural issues\n208\t\t// that prevent proper interpretation of the YAML data.\n209\t\tErrCodeInvalidStructure ErrorCode = \"INVALID_STRUCTURE\"\n210\t\n211\t\t// ErrCodeDuplicateKey indicates that a YAML mapping contains the same key\n212\t\t// multiple times. This is ambiguous and often indicates a configuration error,\n213\t\t// as YAML mappings should have unique keys.\n214\t\tErrCodeDuplicateKey ErrorCode = \"DUPLICATE_KEY\"\n215\t\n216\t\t// ErrCodeParseError indicates a generic parsing error that doesn't fit into\n217\t\t// more specific parse error categories. Use this as a fallback when the exact\n218\t\t// nature of the parse error cannot be determined.\n219\t\tErrCodeParseError ErrorCode = \"PARSE_ERROR\"\n220\t\n221\t\t// ============================================================================\n222\t\t// Validation Error Codes\n223\t\t// ============================================================================\n224\t\n225\t\t// ErrCodeValidationFailed indicates a general validation failure.\n226\t\t// This is used when validation cannot pass but doesn't fit into more specific\n227\t\t// validation error categories.\n228\t\tErrCodeValidationFailed ErrorCode = \"VALIDATION_FAILED\"\n229\t\n230\t\t// ErrCodeRequiredField indicates that a required field is missing from the\n231\t\t// YAML data. This error is used when accessing required fields that don't exist\n232\t\t// in the YAML structure, preventing proper configuration or processing.\n233\t\tErrCodeRequiredField ErrorCode = \"REQUIRED_FIELD\"\n234\t\n235\t\t// ErrCodeConstraintViolation indicates that a field value violates a defined\n236\t\t// constraint. This includes values out of allowed ranges, string length violations,\n237\t\t// pattern matching failures, or any constraint validation rule defined in the\n238\t\t// schema or validation logic.\n239\t\tErrCodeConstraintViolation ErrorCode = \"CONSTRAINT_VIOLATION\"\n240\t\n241\t\t// ErrCodeInvalidValue indicates that a field contains an invalid value that\n242\t\t// doesn't meet validation criteria. This is used when a value is syntactically\n243\t\t// correct but semantically invalid for the intended use.\n244\t\tErrCodeInvalidValue ErrorCode = \"INVALID_VALUE\"\n245\t\n246\t\t// ============================================================================\n247\t\t// Schema Error Codes\n248\t\t// ============================================================================\n249\t\n250\t\t// ErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed indicates an error occurred while loading a schema\n251\t\t// definition. This includes file I/O errors reading the schema file, JSON/YAML\n252\t\t// parsing errors in the schema definition itself, or invalid schema syntax.\n253\t\tErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_LOAD_FAILED\"\n254\t\n255\t\t// ErrCodeSchemaValidation indicates that YAML data failed validation against\n256\t\t// a schema. This error is returned when the YAML structure or content doesn't\n257\t\t// conform to the rules defined in the validation schema.\n258\t\tErrCodeSchemaValidation ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_VALIDATION\"\n259\t\n260\t\t// ErrCodeSchemaNotFound indicates that a required schema file cannot be found.\n261\t\t// This error occurs when the schema reference points to a non-existent file or\n262\t\t// the schema cannot be located in the configured schema paths.\n263\t\tErrCodeSchemaNotFound ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_NOT_FOUND\"\n264\t\n265\t\t// ErrCodeSchemaInvalid indicates that the schema definition itself is invalid.\n266\t\t// This includes schemas with invalid structure, unsupported features, or schemas\n267\t\t// that cannot be properly parsed or interpreted by the validation system.\n268\t\tErrCodeSchemaInvalid ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_INVALID\"\n269\t)\n270\t\n271\t// ParseError represents errors that occur during YAML parsing.\n272\t//\n273\t// ParseError wraps underlying parsing errors and provides detailed\n274\t// location information (line, column) and context about what failed.\n275\ttype ParseError struct {\n276\t\tFilePath   string      // Path to the file being parsed\n277\t\tLine       int         // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n278\t\tColumn     int         // Column number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n279\t\tMessage    string      // Human-readable error message\n280\t\tContextStr string      // Additional context about the parsing state\n281\t\tErr        error       // Underlying error for error wrapping\n282\t\tErrorType  ErrorType   // Specific type of parse error\n283\t\tErrorCode  ErrorCode   // Error code for programmatic handling\n284\t\tExpected   string      // What was expected (optional, for syntax/type errors)\n285\t\tActual     string      // What was actually found (optional, for syntax/type errors)\n286\t}\n287\t\n288\t// Code implements YAMLError interface.\n289\tfunc (pe *ParseError) Code() ErrorCode {\n290\t\tif pe.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n291\t\t\treturn pe.ErrorCode\n292\t\t}\n293\t\treturn ErrCodeParseError\n294\t}\n295\t\n296\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n297\tfunc (pe *ParseError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n298\t\treturn pe.ErrorType\n299\t}\n300\t\n301\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n302\tfunc (pe *ParseError) Context() string {\n303\t\treturn pe.ContextStr\n304\t}\n305\t\n306\t// Error implements the error interface.\n307\tfunc (pe *ParseError) Error() string {\n308\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n309\t\n310\t\t// Build base error with location\n311\t\tif pe.Line > 0 {\n312\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"parse error in %s at line %d\", pe.FilePath, pe.Line))\n313\t\t\tif pe.Column > 0 {\n314\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", pe.Column))\n315\t\t\t}\n316\t\t} else {\n317\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"parse error in %s\", pe.FilePath))\n318\t\t}\n319\t\n320\t\t// Add message\n321\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", pe.Message))\n322\t\n323\t\t// Add expected vs actual if available\n324\t\tif pe.Expected != \"\" || pe.Actual != \"\" {\n325\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\" (\")\n326\t\t\tif pe.Expected != \"\" {\n327\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", pe.Expected))\n328\t\t\t}\n329\t\t\tif pe.Expected != \"\" && pe.Actual != \"\" {\n330\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n331\t\t\t}\n332\t\t\tif pe.Actual != \"\" {\n333\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %s\", pe.Actual))\n334\t\t\t}\n335\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\")\")\n336\t\t}\n337\t\n338\t\treturn sb.String()\n339\t}\n340\t\n341\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\n342\tfunc (pe *ParseError) Unwrap() error {\n343\t\treturn pe.Err\n344\t}\n345\t\n346\t// NewParseError creates a new ParseError with the given parameters.\n347\t//\n348\t// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n349\t// ParseError instances with message, line, column, error code, and optional expected/actual values.\n350\t//\n351\t// Parameters:\n352\t//   - filePath: Path to the file being parsed\n353\t//   - message: Human-readable error message\n354\t//   - line: Line number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n355\t//   - column: Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n356\t//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n357\t//   - expected: What was expected (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n358\t//   - actual: What was actually found (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n359\t//\n360\t// Returns a properly initialized ParseError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n361\t//\n362\t// Example usage:\n363\t//\n364\t//\terr := NewParseError(\"config.yaml\", \"invalid syntax\", 10, 5, ErrCodeInvalidSyntax, \"identifier\", \"123\")\n365\tfunc NewParseError(filePath string, message string, line int, column int, code ErrorCode, expected string, actual string) *ParseError {\n366\t\t// Use provided code or default to generic parse error\n367\t\terrorCode := code\n368\t\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n369\t\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeParseError\n370\t\t}\n371\t\n372\t\treturn &ParseError{\n373\t\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n374\t\t\tMessage:   message,\n375\t\t\tLine:      line,\n376\t\t\tColumn:    column,\n377\t\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n378\t\t\tErrorType: ErrorTypeParse,\n379\t\t\tExpected:  expected,\n380\t\t\tActual:    actual,\n381\t\t}\n382\t}\n383\t\n384\t// IsParseError checks if an error is a ParseError.\n385\tfunc IsParseError(err error) bool {\n386\t\tvar pe *ParseError\n387\t\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &pe) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeParse))\n388\t}\n389\t\n390\t// ValidationError represents errors that occur during YAML validation.\n391\t//\n392\t// ValidationError wraps validation failures and provides detailed\n393\t// information about what failed validation. This is the base error type\n394\t// used throughout the yamlutil package for validation errors.\n395\ttype ValidationError struct {\n396\t\tFilePath     string    // Path to the file being validated\n397\t\tFieldPath    string    // Dot-notation path to the invalid field (optional)\n398\t\tPath         string    // Dot-notation field path (e.g., \"spec.replicas\")\n399\t\tMessage      string    // Human-readable error message\n400\t\tLine         int       // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n401\t\tColumn       int       // Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, optional)\n402\t\tConstraint   string    // Constraint that was violated (optional)\n403\t\tContextStr   string    // Additional context about the validation state (optional)\n404\t\tErr          error     // Underlying error for error wrapping (optional)\n405\t\tErrorCode    ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling (optional)\n406\t\tType         ErrorType // Category of error for type switching\n407\t\tExpectedType string    // Expected type for type mismatch errors (optional)\n408\t\tActualType   string    // Actual type found for type mismatch errors (optional)\n409\t}\n410\t\n411\t// Code implements YAMLError interface.\n412\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) Code() ErrorCode {\n413\t\tif ve.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n414\t\t\treturn ve.ErrorCode\n415\t\t}\n416\t\treturn ErrCodeValidationFailed\n417\t}\n418\t\n419\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n420\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n421\t\t// If Type is explicitly set, use it\n422\t\tif ve.Type != \"\" {\n423\t\t\treturn ve.Type\n424\t\t}\n425\t\t// Try to determine error type from error code or message\n426\t\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeRequiredField || (ve.FieldPath != \"\" && containsRequiredFieldKeywords(ve.Message)) {\n427\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeFieldNotFound\n428\t\t}\n429\t\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeConstraintViolation || containsConstraintKeywords(ve.Message) {\n430\t\t\treturn ErrorTypeConstraint\n431\t\t}\n432\t\treturn ErrorTypeValidation\n433\t}\n434\t\n435\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n436\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) Context() string {\n437\t\treturn ve.ContextStr\n438\t}\n439\t\n440\t// Error implements the error interface.\n441\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) Error() string {\n442\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n443\t\n444\t\t// Build base error with location\n445\t\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n446\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s at line %d\", ve.FilePath, ve.Line))\n447\t\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n448\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", ve.Column))\n449\t\t\t}\n450\t\t} else {\n451\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s\", ve.FilePath))\n452\t\t}\n453\t\n454\t\t// Add field path if available\n455\t\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n456\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" at field %s\", ve.FieldPath))\n457\t\t}\n458\t\n459\t\t// Add message\n460\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", ve.Message))\n461\t\n462\t\t// Add constraint if available\n463\t\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n464\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (constraint: %s)\", ve.Constraint))\n465\t\t}\n466\t\n467\t\t// Add type mismatch information if available\n468\t\tif ve.ExpectedType != \"\" || ve.ActualType != \"\" {\n469\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\" (\")\n470\t\t\tif ve.ExpectedType != \"\" {\n471\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %s\", ve.ExpectedType))\n472\t\t\t}\n473\t\t\tif ve.ExpectedType != \"\" && ve.ActualType != \"\" {\n474\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n475\t\t\t}\n476\t\t\tif ve.ActualType != \"\" {\n477\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"got %s\", ve.ActualType))\n478\t\t\t}\n479\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\")\")\n480\t\t}\n481\t\n482\t\treturn sb.String()\n483\t}\n484\t\n485\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\n486\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) Unwrap() error {\n487\t\treturn ve.Err\n488\t}\n489\t\n490\t// String returns a formatted error message with context.\n491\tfunc (ve *ValidationError) String() string {\n492\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n493\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Error: %s\\n\", ve.Message))\n494\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Type: %s\\n\", ve.YAMLErrorType()))\n495\t\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n496\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Location: Line %d\", ve.Line))\n497\t\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n498\t\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", Column %d\", ve.Column))\n499\t\t\t}\n500\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n501\t\t}\n502\t\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n503\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Field: %s\\n\", ve.FieldPath))\n504\t\t}\n505\t\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n506\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Constraint: %s\\n\", ve.Constraint))\n507\t\t}\n508\t\tif ve.ExpectedType != \"\" {\n509\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Expected Type: %s\\n\", ve.ExpectedType))\n510\t\t}\n511\t\tif ve.ActualType != \"\" {\n512\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Actual Type: %s\\n\", ve.ActualType))\n513\t\t}\n514\t\tif ve.ContextStr != \"\" {\n515\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context: %s\\n\", ve.ContextStr))\n516\t\t}\n517\t\treturn sb.String()\n518\t}\n519\t\n520\t// NewValidationError creates a new ValidationError with the given parameters.\n521\t//\n522\t// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n523\t// ValidationError instances with message, path, constraint, error code, and location.\n524\t//\n525\t// Parameters:\n526\t//   - filePath: Path to the file being validated\n527\t//   - message: Human-readable error message\n528\t//   - fieldPath: Dot-notation path to the invalid field (optional)\n529\t//   - constraint: Constraint that was violated (optional)\n530\t//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n531\t//   - line: Line number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n532\t//   - column: Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n533\t//   - errorType: Category of error (use empty string for default ErrorTypeValidation)\n534\t//   - path: Dot-notation field path (optional, for backward compatibility defaults to empty string)\n535\t//\n536\t// Returns a properly initialized ValidationError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n537\t//\n538\t// Example usage:\n539\t//\n540\t//\terr := NewValidationError(\"config.yaml\", \"invalid port number\", \"server.port\", \"must be between 1-65535\", ErrCodeInvalidValue, 10, 5, \"\", \"spec.replicas\")\n541\tfunc NewValidationError(filePath string, message string, fieldPath string, constraint string, code ErrorCode, line int, column int, errorType ErrorType, path string) *ValidationError {\n542\t\t// Use provided code or default to generic validation error\n543\t\terrorCode := code\n544\t\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n545\t\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeValidationFailed\n546\t\t}\n547\t\n548\t\t// Use provided error type or default to validation error\n549\t\teType := errorType\n550\t\tif eType == \"\" {\n551\t\t\teType = ErrorTypeValidation\n552\t\t}\n553\t\n554\t\t// Handle nil/empty path: use fieldPath as fallback if path is empty\n555\t\t// This ensures the Path field is populated when available\n556\t\tvalidPath := path\n557\t\tif validPath == \"\" && fieldPath != \"\" {\n558\t\t\tvalidPath = fieldPath\n559\t\t}\n560\t\n561\t\treturn &ValidationError{\n562\t\t\tFilePath:   filePath,\n563\t\t\tMessage:    message,\n564\t\t\tFieldPath:  fieldPath,\n565\t\t\tConstraint: constraint,\n566\t\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n567\t\t\tLine:       line,\n568\t\t\tColumn:     column,\n569\t\t\tType:       eType,\n570\t\t\tPath:       validPath,\n571\t\t}\n572\t}\n573\t\n574\t// IsValidationError checks if an error is a ValidationError.\n575\tfunc IsValidationError(err error) bool {\n576\t\tvar ve *ValidationError\n577\t\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &ve) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeValidation))\n578\t}\n579\t\n580\t// NewFileError creates a new FileError with proper initialization.\n581\tfunc NewFileError(path string, operation string, message string, errorCode ErrorCode) *FileError {\n582\t\treturn &FileError{\n583\t\t\tPath:       path,\n584\t\t\tOperation:  operation,\n585\t\t\tMessage:    message,\n586\t\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n587\t\t}\n588\t}\n589\t\n590\t// NewSchemaValidationError creates a new SchemaValidationError with proper StructureError with proper initialization.\n655\tfunc NewStructureError(filePath string, message string, line int, duplicateKey string, location string, errorCode ErrorCode) *StructureError {\n656\t\treturn &StructureError{\n657\t\t\tFilePath:     filePath,\n658\t\t\tMessage:      message,\n659\t\t\tLine:         line,\n660\t\t\tDuplicateKey: duplicateKey,\n661\t\t\tLocation:     location,\n662\t\t\tErrorCode:    errorCode,\n663\t\t}\n664\t}\n665\t\n666\t// NewDuplicateKeyError creates a new DuplicateKeyError with proper initialization.\n667\t//\n668\t// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n669\t// DuplicateKeyError instances with the given parameters.\n670\t//\n671\t// Parameters:\n672\t//   - filePath: Path to the file first occurrence (use 0 if unknown)\n676\t//   - line2: Line number of duplicate occurrence (use 0 if unknown)\n677\t//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n678\t//\n679\t// Returns a properly initialized DuplicateKeyError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n680\tfunc NewDuplicateKeyError(filePath string, key string, location string, line1 int, line2 int, code ErrorCode) *DuplicateKeyError {\n681\t\t// Use provided code or default to duplicate key error\n682\t\terrorCode := code\n683\t\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n684\t\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeDuplicateKey\n685\t\t}\n686\t\n687\t\treturn &DuplicateKeyError{\n688\t\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n689\t\t\tKey:       key,\n690\t\t\tLocation:  location,\n691\t\t\tLine1:     line1,\n692\t\t\tLine2:     line2,\n693\t\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n694\t\t}\n695\t}\n696\t\n697\t// NewSchemaLoadError creates a new SchemaLoadError with proper initialization.\n698\t//\n699\t// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n700\t// SchemaLoadError instances with the given parameters.\n701\t//\n702\t// Parameters:\n703\t//   - filePath: Path to the schema file\n704\t//   - message: Description of the load error\n705\t//   - err: Underlying error (optional, use nil if not applicable)\n706\t//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n707\t//\n708\t// Returns a properly initialized SchemaLoadError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n709\tfunc NewSchemaLoadError(filePath string, message string, err error, code ErrorCode) *SchemaLoadError {\n710\t\t// Use provided code or default to schema load failed error\n711\t\terrorCode := code\n712\t\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n713\t\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed\n714\t\t}\n715\t\n716\t\treturn &SchemaLoadError{\n717\t\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n718\t\t\tMessage:   message,\n719\t\t\tErr:       err,\n720\t\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n721\t\t}\n722\t}\n723\t\n724\t// containsRequiredFieldKeywords checks if the message suggests a required field error.\n725\tfunc containsRequiredFieldKeywords(msg string) bool {\n726\t\tkeywords := []string{\"required\", \"missing\", \"not found\", \"must be provided\"}\n727\t\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n728\t\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n729\t\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n730\t\t\t\treturn true\n731\t\t\t}\n732\t\t}\n733\t\treturn false\n734\t}\n735\t\n736\t// containsConstraintKeywords checks if the message suggests a constraint violation.\n737\tfunc containsConstraintKeywords(msg string) bool {\n738\t\tkeywords := []string{\"constraint\", \"range\", \"length\", \"pattern\", \"invalid value\"}\n739\t\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n740\t\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n741\t\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n742\t\t\t\treturn true\n743\t\t\t}\n744\t\t}\n745\t\treturn false\n746\t}\n747\t\n748\t// FileError represents errors that occur during file I/O operations.\n749\t//\n750\t// FileError wraps file I/O errors and provides detailed\n751\t// information about the file operation that failed.\n752\ttype FileError struct {\n753\t\tOp        string    // Operation that failed (read, write, etc.) - \"Op\" for backward compatibility\n754\t\tPath      string    // Path to the file\n755\t\tOperation string    // Operation that failed (read, write, etc.) - \"Operation\" for clarity\n756\t\tMessage   string    // Human-readable error message\n757\t\tErr       error     // Underlying error for error wrapping\n758\t\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n759\t}\n760\t\n761\t// Code implements YAMLError interface.\n762\tfunc (fe *FileError) Code() ErrorCode {\n763\t\tif fe.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n764\t\t\treturn fe.ErrorCode\n765\t\t}\n766\t\treturn ErrCodeFileIOError\n767\t}\n768\t\n769\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n770\tfunc (fe *FileError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n771\t\treturn ErrorTypeFile\n772\t}\n773\t\n774\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n775\tfunc (fe *FileError) Context() string {\n776\t\top := fe.Operation\n777\t\tif op == \"\" && fe.Op != \"\" {\n778\t\t\top = fe.Op\n779\t\t}\n780\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"operation: %s, path: %s\", op, fe.Path)\n781\t}\n782\t\n783\t// Error implements the error interface.\n784\tfunc (fe *FileError) Error() string {\n785\t\top := fe.Operation\n786\t\tif op == \"\" && fe.Op != \"\" {\n787\t\t\top = fe.Op\n788\t\t}\n789\t\n790\t\tvar sb strings.Builder\n791\t\tif op != \"\" {\n792\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"file error during %s on %s\", op, fe.Path))\n793\t\t} else {\n794\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"file error in %s\", fe.Path))\n795\t\t}\n796\t\n797\t\tif fe.Message != \"\" {\n798\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", fe.Message))\n799\t\t}\n800\t\n801\t\tif fe.Err != nil {\n802\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", fe.Err.Error()))\n803\t\t}\n804\t\n805\t\treturn sb.String()\n806\t}\n807\t\n808\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\n809\tfunc (fe *FileError) Unwrap() error {\n810\t\treturn fe.Err\n811\t}\n812\t\n813\t// IsFileError checks if an error is a FileError.\n814\tfunc IsFileError(err error) bool {\n815\t\tvar fe *FileError\n816\t\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &fe) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeFile))\n817\t}\n818\t\n819\t// SyntaxError represents YAML syntax errors during parsing.\n820\t//\n821\t// SyntaxError includes indentation errors, malformed YAML, and other\n822\t// syntax-related issues that prevent the YAML from being parsed.\n823\ttype SyntaxError struct {\n824\t\tFilePath  string    // Path to the file with syntax error\n825\t\tLine      int       // Line number where syntax error occurred\n826\t\tColumn    int       // Column number where syntax error occurred\n827\t\tMessage   string    // Description of the syntax error\n828\t\tExpected  string    // What was expected (if known)\n829\t\tFound     string    // What was actually found (if known)\n830\t\tErr       error     // Underlying yaml parser error\n831\t\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n832\t}\n833\t\n834\t// Code implements YAMLError interface.\n835\tfunc (se *SyntaxError) Code() ErrorCode {\n836\t\tif se.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n837\t\t\treturn se.ErrorCode\n838\t\t}\n839\t\treturn ErrCodeInvalidSyntax\n840\t}\n841\t\n842\t// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\n843\tfunc (se *SyntaxError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n844\t\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n845\t}\n846\t\n847\t// Context implements YAMLError interface.\n848\tfunc (se *SyntaxError) Context() string {\n849\t\tctx := fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", se.Expected)\n850\t\tif se.Found != \"\" {\n851\t\t\tctx += fmt.Sprintf(\", found: %s\", se.Found)\n852\t\t}\n853\t\treturn ctx\n854\t}\n855\t\n856\t// Error implements the error interface.\n857\tfunc (se *SyntaxError) Error() string {\n858\t\tif se.Line > 0 {\n859\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"syntax error in %s at line %d, column %d: %s\", se.FilePath, se.Line, se.Column, se.Message)\n860\t\t}\n861\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"syntax error in %s: %s\", se.FilePath, se.Message)\n862\t}\n863\t\n864\t// Unwrap returns the underlying error.\n865\tfunc (se *SyntaxError) Unwrap() error {\n866\t\treturn se.Err\n867\t}\n868\t\n869\t// StructureError represents YAML Package yamlutil provides comprehensive error type definitions for YAML processing.\n//\n// Error Hierarchy:\n//\n//\tYAMLError (base interface)\n//\t├── FileError (file I/O errors)\n//\t├── ParseError (YAML parsing errors)\n//\t│   ├── SyntaxError (YAML syntax errors)\n//\t│   ├── StructureError (YAML structure errors)\n//\t│   └── TypeMismatchError (type conversion errors)\n//\t├── ValidationError (validation errors)\n//\t│   ├── FieldNotFoundError (missing required fields)\n//\t│   ├── ConstraintError (constraint violations)\n//\t│   └── DuplicateKeyError (duplicate key errors)\n//\t└── SchemaError (schema-related errors)\n//\t\t├── SchemaLoadError (schema loading errors)\n//\t\t└── SchemaValidationError (schema validation errors)\npackage yamlutil\n\nimport (\n\t\"errors\"\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"os\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// YAMLError is the base interface for all YAML processing errors.\n//\n// This interface provides a common type for all errors that occur during\n// YAML file I/O, parsing, validation, and schema operations.\ntype YAMLError interface {\n\terror\n\n\t// Code returns the error code for programmatic error handling.\n\tCode() ErrorCode\n\n\t// YAMLErrorType returns the category of error for type switching.\n\tYAMLErrorType() ErrorType\n\n\t// Context returns additional context about the error.\n\tContext() string\n}\n\n// ErrorType represents the category of YAML error.\ntype ErrorType string\n\nconst (\n\tErrorTypeFile           ErrorType = \"file\"           // File I/O errors\n\tErrorTypeParse          ErrorType = \"parse\"          // YAML parsing errors\n\tErrorTypeSyntax         ErrorType = \"syntax\"         // YAML syntax errors\n\tErrorTypeStructure      ErrorType = \"structure\"      // YAML structure errors\n\tErrorTypeTypeMismatch   ErrorType = \"type_mismatch\"  // Type conversion errors\n\tErrorTypeValidation     ErrorType = \"validation\"     // Validation errors\n\tErrorTypeFieldNotFound  ErrorType = \"field_not_found\" // Missing required fields\n\tErrorTypeConstraint     ErrorType = \"constraint\"     // Constraint violations\n\tErrorTypeDuplicateKey   ErrorType = \"duplicate_key\"  // Duplicate key errors\n\tErrorTypeSchema         ErrorType = \"schema\"         // Schema-related errors\n\tErrorTypeSchemaLoad     ErrorType = \"schema_load\"     // Schema loading errors\n\tErrorTypeSchemaValidate ErrorType = \"schema_validate\" // Schema validation errors\n\tErrorTypeUnknown        ErrorType = \"unknown\"        // Unknown error type\n\tErrorTypeEmpty          ErrorType = \"empty\"          // Empty content\n\tErrorTypeIO             ErrorType = \"io\"             // I/O error\n)\n\n// ParseErrorVariant represents the specific variant/category of a parse error.\n//\n// ParseErrorVariant provides a structured way to categorize different types of\n// parse errors, enabling precise error handling and user-friendly error messages.\n// Each variant represents a distinct category of parsing failure.\ntype ParseErrorVariant string\n\nconst (\n\t// ParseErrorVariantSyntax represents YAML syntax errors.\n\t//\n\t// This variant covers fundamental YAML syntax issues including:\n\t// - Invalid indentation\n\t// - Malformed YAML structure\n\t// - Invalid escape sequences\n\t// - Improper quoting\n\t// - Invalid YAML tokens\n\tParseErrorVariantSyntax ParseErrorVariant = \"syntax\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch represents type conversion errors.\n\t//\n\t// This variant covers errors when YAML content cannot be converted to the\n\t// expected Go type, including:\n\t// - Attempting to parse a string as an integer\n\t// - Trying to unmarshal a scalar into a struct\n\t// - Incompatible array/slice types\n\t// - Invalid boolean representations\n\tParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch ParseErrorVariant = \"type_mismatch\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorVariantValidation represents constraint validation errors.\n\t//\n\t// This variant covers errors when YAML data violates defined constraints,\n\t// including:\n\t// - Value out of allowed range\n\t// - String length violations\n\t// - Pattern matching failures\n\t// - Required field violations\n\tParseErrorVariantValidation ParseErrorVariant = \"validation\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorVariantIO represents I/O operation errors.\n\t//\n\t// This variant covers errors related to file operations during parsing,\n\t// including:\n\t// - File not found\n\t// - Permission denied\n\t// - Read/write failures\n\t// - File system errors\n\tParseErrorVariantIO ParseErrorVariant = \"io\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorVariantStructure represents YAML structure errors.\n\t//\n\t// This variant covers structural issues that syntax checking alone won't catch,\n\t// including:\n\t// - Duplicate mapping keys\n\t// - Invalid nesting\n\t// - Circular references\n\t// - Inconsistent document structure\n\tParseErrorVariantStructure ParseErrorVariant = \"structure\"\n\n\t// ParseErrorVariantCustom represents custom/extensible error categories.\n\t//\n\t// This variant is reserved for application-specific parsing errors that don't\n\t// fit into the standard categories. It enables extensibility for domain-specific\n\t// validation rules and custom parsing logic.\n\t// Applications can use this variant with custom error codes and messages\n\t// to represent specialized error conditions.\n\tParseErrorVariantCustom ParseErrorVariant = \"custom\"\n)\n\n// String returns the string representation of the ParseErrorVariant.\nfunc (v ParseErrorVariant) String() string {\n\treturn string(v)\n}\n\n// Description returns a human-readable description of the ParseErrorVariant.\nfunc (v ParseErrorVariant) Description() string {\n\tswitch v {\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantSyntax:\n\t\treturn \"YAML syntax error - invalid YAML structure or formatting\"\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantTypeMismatch:\n\t\treturn \"Type mismatch error - cannot convert YAML to expected type\"\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantValidation:\n\t\treturn \"Validation error - constraint violation or invalid value\"\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantIO:\n\t\treturn \"I/O error - file operation failed\"\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantStructure:\n\t\treturn \"Structure error - invalid YAML document structure\"\n\tcase ParseErrorVariantCustom:\n\t\treturn \"Custom error - application-specific parsing error\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn \"Unknown parse error variant\"\n\t}\n}\n\n// ErrorCode represents specific error codes for programmatic error handling.\n//\n// ErrorCodes provide machine-readable identifiers for errors, enabling\n// programmatic error handling and internationalization of error messages.\ntype ErrorCode string\n\nconst (\n\t// ============================================================================\n\t// File Error Codes\n\t// ============================================================================\n\n\t// ErrCodeFileNotFound indicates that a file does not exist at the specified path.\n\t// This error is returned when file operations fail because the target file cannot\n\t// be found in the filesystem.\n\tErrCodeFileNotFound ErrorCode = \"FILE_NOT_FOUND\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeFileAccessDenied indicates insufficient permissions to access a file.\n\t// This error occurs when the application lacks the necessary read or write\n\t// permissions for the specified file path.\n\tErrCodeFileAccessDenied ErrorCode = \"FILE_ACCESS_DENIED\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeFileIOError indicates a generic file I/O error that doesn't fit\n\t// other specific file error categories. This includes disk errors, filesystem\n\t// issues, or other low-level I/O failures.\n\tErrCodeFileIOError ErrorCode = \"FILE_IO_ERROR\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeFileEmpty indicates that a file exists but contains no data or\n\t// only whitespace. This error is used when parsing an empty file would result\n\t// in undefined behavior or missing required configuration.\n\tErrCodeFileEmpty ErrorCode = \"FILE_EMPTY\"\n\n\t// ============================================================================\n\t// Parse Error Codes\n\t// ============================================================================\n\n\t// ErrCodeInvalidSyntax indicates a YAML syntax error during parsing.\n\t// This includes invalid indentation, malformed YAML structure, invalid escape\n\t// sequences, improper quoting, or any fundamental YAML syntax violation that\n\t// prevents the document from being parsed.\n\tErrCodeInvalidSyntax ErrorCode = \"INVALID_SYNTAX\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeTypeMismatch indicates a type conversion error during parsing.\n\t// This occurs when YAML content cannot be converted to the expected Go type,\n\t// such as attempting to parse a string as an integer, unmarshaling a scalar into\n\t// a struct, or incompatible array/slice types.\n\tErrCodeTypeMismatch ErrorCode = \"TYPE_MISMATCH\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeInvalidStructure indicates a YAML structure error that passes\n\t// syntax checking but represents invalid document structure. This includes\n\t// invalid nesting, inconsistent document structure, or other structural issues\n\t// that prevent proper interpretation of the YAML data.\n\tErrCodeInvalidStructure ErrorCode = \"INVALID_STRUCTURE\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeDuplicateKey indicates that a YAML mapping contains the same key\n\t// multiple times. This is ambiguous and often indicates a configuration error,\n\t// as YAML mappings should have unique keys.\n\tErrCodeDuplicateKey ErrorCode = \"DUPLICATE_KEY\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeParseError indicates a generic parsing error that doesn't fit into\n\t// more specific parse error categories. Use this as a fallback when the exact\n\t// nature of the parse error cannot be determined.\n\tErrCodeParseError ErrorCode = \"PARSE_ERROR\"\n\n\t// ============================================================================\n\t// Validation Error Codes\n\t// ============================================================================\n\n\t// ErrCodeValidationFailed indicates a general validation failure.\n\t// This is used when validation cannot pass but doesn't fit into more specific\n\t// validation error categories.\n\tErrCodeValidationFailed ErrorCode = \"VALIDATION_FAILED\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeRequiredField indicates that a required field is missing from the\n\t// YAML data. This error is used when accessing required fields that don't exist\n\t// in the YAML structure, preventing proper configuration or processing.\n\tErrCodeRequiredField ErrorCode = \"REQUIRED_FIELD\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeConstraintViolation indicates that a field value violates a defined\n\t// constraint. This includes values out of allowed ranges, string length violations,\n\t// pattern matching failures, or any constraint validation rule defined in the\n\t// schema or validation logic.\n\tErrCodeConstraintViolation ErrorCode = \"CONSTRAINT_VIOLATION\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeInvalidValue indicates that a field contains an invalid value that\n\t// doesn't meet validation criteria. This is used when a value is syntactically\n\t// correct but semantically invalid for the intended use.\n\tErrCodeInvalidValue ErrorCode = \"INVALID_VALUE\"\n\n\t// ============================================================================\n\t// Schema Error Codes\n\t// ============================================================================\n\n\t// ErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed indicates an error occurred while loading a schema\n\t// definition. This includes file I/O errors reading the schema file, JSON/YAML\n\t// parsing errors in the schema definition itself, or invalid schema syntax.\n\tErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_LOAD_FAILED\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeSchemaValidation indicates that YAML data failed validation against\n\t// a schema. This error is returned when the YAML structure or content doesn't\n\t// conform to the rules defined in the validation schema.\n\tErrCodeSchemaValidation ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_VALIDATION\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeSchemaNotFound indicates that a required schema file cannot be found.\n\t// This error occurs when the schema reference points to a non-existent file or\n\t// the schema cannot be located in the configured schema paths.\n\tErrCodeSchemaNotFound ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_NOT_FOUND\"\n\n\t// ErrCodeSchemaInvalid indicates that the schema definition itself is invalid.\n\t// This includes schemas with invalid structure, unsupported features, or schemas\n\t// that cannot be properly parsed or interpreted by the validation system.\n\tErrCodeSchemaInvalid ErrorCode = \"SCHEMA_INVALID\"\n)\n\n// ParseError represents errors that occur during YAML parsing.\n//\n// ParseError wraps underlying parsing errors and provides detailed\n// location information (line, column) and context about what failed.\ntype ParseError struct {\n\tFilePath   string      // Path to the file being parsed\n\tLine       int         // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tColumn     int         // Column number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tMessage    string      // Human-readable error message\n\tContextStr string      // Additional context about the parsing state\n\tErr        error       // Underlying error for error wrapping\n\tErrorType  ErrorType   // Specific type of parse error\n\tErrorCode  ErrorCode   // Error code for programmatic handling\n\tExpected   string      // What was expected (optional, for syntax/type errors)\n\tActual     string      // What was actually found (optional, for syntax/type errors)\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif pe.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn pe.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeParseError\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn pe.ErrorType\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Context() string {\n\treturn pe.ContextStr\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Error() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\t// Build base error with location\n\tif pe.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"parse error in %s at line %d\", pe.FilePath, pe.Line))\n\t\tif pe.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", pe.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"parse error in %s\", pe.FilePath))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add message\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", pe.Message))\n\n\t// Add expected vs actual if available\n\tif pe.Expected != \"\" || pe.Actual != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\" (\")\n\t\tif pe.Expected != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", pe.Expected))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif pe.Expected != \"\" && pe.Actual != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tif pe.Actual != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %s\", pe.Actual))\n\t\t}\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\")\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\nfunc (pe *ParseError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn pe.Err\n}\n\n// NewParseError creates a new ParseError with the given parameters.\n//\n// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n// ParseError instances with message, line, column, error code, and optional expected/actual values.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - filePath: Path to the file being parsed\n//   - message: Human-readable error message\n//   - line: Line number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n//   - column: Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n//   - expected: What was expected (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n//   - actual: What was actually found (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n//\n// Returns a properly initialized ParseError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := NewParseError(\"config.yaml\", \"invalid syntax\", 10, 5, ErrCodeInvalidSyntax, \"identifier\", \"123\")\nfunc NewParseError(filePath string, message string, line int, column int, code ErrorCode, expected string, actual string) *ParseError {\n\t// Use provided code or default to generic parse error\n\terrorCode := code\n\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeParseError\n\t}\n\n\treturn &ParseError{\n\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n\t\tMessage:   message,\n\t\tLine:      line,\n\t\tColumn:    column,\n\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n\t\tErrorType: ErrorTypeParse,\n\t\tExpected:  expected,\n\t\tActual:    actual,\n\t}\n}\n\n// IsParseError checks if an error is a ParseError.\nfunc IsParseError(err error) bool {\n\tvar pe *ParseError\n\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &pe) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeParse))\n}\n\n// ValidationError represents errors that occur during YAML validation.\n//\n// ValidationError wraps validation failures and provides detailed\n// information about what failed validation. This is the base error type\n// used throughout the yamlutil package for validation errors.\ntype ValidationError struct {\n\tFilePath     string    // Path to the file being validated\n\tFieldPath    string    // Dot-notation path to the invalid field (optional)\n\tPath         string    // Dot-notation field path (e.g., \"spec.replicas\")\n\tMessage      string    // Human-readable error message\n\tLine         int       // Line number where error occurred (1-indexed)\n\tColumn       int       // Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, optional)\n\tConstraint   string    // Constraint that was violated (optional)\n\tContextStr   string    // Additional context about the validation state (optional)\n\tErr          error     // Underlying error for error wrapping (optional)\n\tErrorCode    ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling (optional)\n\tType         ErrorType // Category of error for type switching\n\tExpectedType string    // Expected type for type mismatch errors (optional)\n\tActualType   string    // Actual type found for type mismatch errors (optional)\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif ve.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ve.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeValidationFailed\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\t// If Type is explicitly set, use it\n\tif ve.Type != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ve.Type\n\t}\n\t// Try to determine error type from error code or message\n\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeRequiredField || (ve.FieldPath != \"\" && containsRequiredFieldKeywords(ve.Message)) {\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeFieldNotFound\n\t}\n\tif ve.ErrorCode == ErrCodeConstraintViolation || containsConstraintKeywords(ve.Message) {\n\t\treturn ErrorTypeConstraint\n\t}\n\treturn ErrorTypeValidation\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Context() string {\n\treturn ve.ContextStr\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Error() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\n\t// Build base error with location\n\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s at line %d\", ve.FilePath, ve.Line))\n\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", column %d\", ve.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t} else {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"validation error in %s\", ve.FilePath))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add field path if available\n\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" at field %s\", ve.FieldPath))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add message\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", ve.Message))\n\n\t// Add constraint if available\n\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" (constraint: %s)\", ve.Constraint))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add type mismatch information if available\n\tif ve.ExpectedType != \"\" || ve.ActualType != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\" (\")\n\t\tif ve.ExpectedType != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %s\", ve.ExpectedType))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif ve.ExpectedType != \"\" && ve.ActualType != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tif ve.ActualType != \"\" {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"got %s\", ve.ActualType))\n\t\t}\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\")\")\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn ve.Err\n}\n\n// String returns a formatted error message with context.\nfunc (ve *ValidationError) String() string {\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Error: %s\\n\", ve.Message))\n\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Type: %s\\n\", ve.YAMLErrorType()))\n\tif ve.Line > 0 {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Location: Line %d\", ve.Line))\n\t\tif ve.Column > 0 {\n\t\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\", Column %d\", ve.Column))\n\t\t}\n\t\tsb.WriteString(\"\\n\")\n\t}\n\tif ve.FieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Field: %s\\n\", ve.FieldPath))\n\t}\n\tif ve.Constraint != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Constraint: %s\\n\", ve.Constraint))\n\t}\n\tif ve.ExpectedType != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Expected Type: %s\\n\", ve.ExpectedType))\n\t}\n\tif ve.ActualType != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Actual Type: %s\\n\", ve.ActualType))\n\t}\n\tif ve.ContextStr != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"  Context: %s\\n\", ve.ContextStr))\n\t}\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// NewValidationError creates a new ValidationError with the given parameters.\n//\n// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n// ValidationError instances with message, path, constraint, error code, and location.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - filePath: Path to the file being validated\n//   - message: Human-readable error message\n//   - fieldPath: Dot-notation path to the invalid field (optional)\n//   - constraint: Constraint that was violated (optional)\n//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n//   - line: Line number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n//   - column: Column number where error occurred (1-indexed, use 0 if unknown)\n//   - errorType: Category of error (use empty string for default ErrorTypeValidation)\n//   - path: Dot-notation field path (optional, for backward compatibility defaults to empty string)\n//\n// Returns a properly initialized ValidationError that implements the YAMLError interface.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := NewValidationError(\"config.yaml\", \"invalid port number\", \"server.port\", \"must be between 1-65535\", ErrCodeInvalidValue, 10, 5, \"\", \"spec.replicas\")\nfunc NewValidationError(filePath string, message string, fieldPath string, constraint string, code ErrorCode, line int, column int, errorType ErrorType, path string) *ValidationError {\n\t// Use provided code or default to generic validation error\n\terrorCode := code\n\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeValidationFailed\n\t}\n\n\t// Use provided error type or default to validation error\n\teType := errorType\n\tif eType == \"\" {\n\t\teType = ErrorTypeValidation\n\t}\n\n\t// Handle nil/empty path: use fieldPath as fallback if path is empty\n\t// This ensures the Path field is populated when available\n\tvalidPath := path\n\tif validPath == \"\" && fieldPath != \"\" {\n\t\tvalidPath = fieldPath\n\t}\n\n\treturn &ValidationError{\n\t\tFilePath:   filePath,\n\t\tMessage:    message,\n\t\tFieldPath:  fieldPath,\n\t\tConstraint: constraint,\n\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n\t\tLine:       line,\n\t\tColumn:     column,\n\t\tType:       eType,\n\t\tPath:       validPath,\n\t}\n}\n\n// IsValidationError checks if an error is a ValidationError.\nfunc IsValidationError(err error) bool {\n\tvar ve *ValidationError\n\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &ve) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeValidation))\n}\n\n// NewFileError creates a new FileError with proper initialization.\nfunc NewFileError(path string, operation string, message string, errorCode ErrorCode) *FileError {\n\treturn &FileError{\n\t\tPath:       path,\n\t\tOperation:  operation,\n\t\tMessage:    message,\n\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewSchemaValidationError creates a new SchemaValidationError with proper initialization.\nfunc NewSchemaValidationError(filePath string, schemaPath string, fieldPath string, message string, expected string, found string, line int, errorCode ErrorCode) *SchemaValidationError {\n\treturn &SchemaValidationError{\n\t\tFilePath:   filePath,\n\t\tSchemaPath: schemaPath,\n\t\tFieldPath:  fieldPath,\n\t\tMessage:    message,\n\t\tExpected:   expected,\n\t\tFound:      found,\n\t\tLine:       line,\n\t\tErrorCode:  errorCode,\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewTypeMismatchError creates a new TypeMismatchError with proper initialization.\nfunc NewTypeMismatchError(filePath string, fieldPath string, expectedType string, actualType string, value string, line int, errorCode ErrorCode) *TypeMismatchError {\n\treturn &TypeMismatchError{\n\t\tFilePath:     filePath,\n\t\tFieldPath:    fieldPath,\n\t\tExpectedType: expectedType,\n\t\tActualType:   actualType,\n\t\tValue:        value,\n\t\tLine:         line,\n\t\tErrorCode:    errorCode,\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewFieldNotFoundError creates a new FieldNotFoundError with proper initialization.\nfunc NewFieldNotFoundError(filePath string, fieldPath string, line int, errorCode ErrorCode) *FieldNotFoundError {\n\treturn &FieldNotFoundError{\n\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n\t\tFieldPath: fieldPath,\n\t\tLine:      line,\n\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewConstraintError creates a new ConstraintError with proper initialization.\nfunc NewConstraintError(filePath string, fieldPath string, constraintType string, constraint string, message string, value string, line int, errorCode ErrorCode) *ConstraintError {\n\treturn &ConstraintError{\n\t\tFilePath:       filePath,\n\t\tFieldPath:      fieldPath,\n\t\tMessage:        message,\n\t\tConstraintType: constraintType,\n\t\tConstraint:     constraint,\n\t\tValue:          value,\n\t\tLine:           line,\n\t\tErrorCode:      errorCode,\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewSyntaxError creates a new SyntaxError with proper initialization.\nfunc NewSyntaxError(filePath string, message string, line int, column int, expected string, found string, errorCode ErrorCode) *SyntaxError {\n\treturn &SyntaxError{\n\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n\t\tMessage:   message,\n\t\tLine:      line,\n\t\tColumn:    column,\n\t\tExpected:  expected,\n\t\tFound:     found,\n\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewStructureError creates a new StructureError with proper initialization.\nfunc NewStructureError(filePath string, message string, line int, duplicateKey string, location string, errorCode ErrorCode) *StructureError {\n\treturn &StructureError{\n\t\tFilePath:     filePath,\n\t\tMessage:      message,\n\t\tLine:         line,\n\t\tDuplicateKey: duplicateKey,\n\t\tLocation:     location,\n\t\tErrorCode:    errorCode,\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewDuplicateKeyError creates a new DuplicateKeyError with proper initialization.\n//\n// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n// DuplicateKeyError instances with the given parameters.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - filePath: Path to the file with duplicate keys\n//   - key: The duplicate key name\n//   - location: Nested path to the duplicate key (optional, use empty string if not applicable)\n//   - line1: Line number of first occurrence (use 0 if unknown)\n//   - line2: Line number of duplicate occurrence (use 0 if unknown)\n//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n//\n// Returns a properly initialized DuplicateKeyError that implements the YAMLError interface.\nfunc NewDuplicateKeyError(filePath string, key string, location string, line1 int, line2 int, code ErrorCode) *DuplicateKeyError {\n\t// Use provided code or default to duplicate key error\n\terrorCode := code\n\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeDuplicateKey\n\t}\n\n\treturn &DuplicateKeyError{\n\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n\t\tKey:       key,\n\t\tLocation:  location,\n\t\tLine1:     line1,\n\t\tLine2:     line2,\n\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n\t}\n}\n\n// NewSchemaLoadError creates a new SchemaLoadError with proper initialization.\n//\n// This constructor function provides a convenient way to create properly initialized\n// SchemaLoadError instances with the given parameters.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - filePath: Path to the schema file\n//   - message: Description of the load error\n//   - err: Underlying error (optional, use nil if not applicable)\n//   - code: Error code for programmatic handling (use empty string for default)\n//\n// Returns a properly initialized SchemaLoadError that implements the YAMLError interface.\nfunc NewSchemaLoadError(filePath string, message string, err error, code ErrorCode) *SchemaLoadError {\n\t// Use provided code or default to schema load failed error\n\terrorCode := code\n\tif errorCode == \"\" {\n\t\terrorCode = ErrCodeSchemaLoadFailed\n\t}\n\n\treturn &SchemaLoadError{\n\t\tFilePath:  filePath,\n\t\tMessage:   message,\n\t\tErr:       err,\n\t\tErrorCode: errorCode,\n\t}\n}\n\n// containsRequiredFieldKeywords checks if the message suggests a required field error.\nfunc containsRequiredFieldKeywords(msg string) bool {\n\tkeywords := []string{\"required\", \"missing\", \"not found\", \"must be provided\"}\n\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// containsConstraintKeywords checks if the message suggests a constraint violation.\nfunc containsConstraintKeywords(msg string) bool {\n\tkeywords := []string{\"constraint\", \"range\", \"length\", \"pattern\", \"invalid value\"}\n\tlower := strings.ToLower(msg)\n\tfor _, kw := range keywords {\n\t\tif strings.Contains(lower, kw) {\n\t\t\treturn true\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn false\n}\n\n// FileError represents errors that occur during file I/O operations.\n//\n// FileError wraps file I/O errors and provides detailed\n// information about the file operation that failed.\ntype FileError struct {\n\tOp        string    // Operation that failed (read, write, etc.) - \"Op\" for backward compatibility\n\tPath      string    // Path to the file\n\tOperation string    // Operation that failed (read, write, etc.) - \"Operation\" for clarity\n\tMessage   string    // Human-readable error message\n\tErr       error     // Underlying error for error wrapping\n\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (fe *FileError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif fe.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fe.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeFileIOError\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (fe *FileError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeFile\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (fe *FileError) Context() string {\n\top := fe.Operation\n\tif op == \"\" && fe.Op != \"\" {\n\t\top = fe.Op\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"operation: %s, path: %s\", op, fe.Path)\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (fe *FileError) Error() string {\n\top := fe.Operation\n\tif op == \"\" && fe.Op != \"\" {\n\t\top = fe.Op\n\t}\n\n\tvar sb strings.Builder\n\tif op != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"file error during %s on %s\", op, fe.Path))\n\t} else {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"file error in %s\", fe.Path))\n\t}\n\n\tif fe.Message != \"\" {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", fe.Message))\n\t}\n\n\tif fe.Err != nil {\n\t\tsb.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\": %s\", fe.Err.Error()))\n\t}\n\n\treturn sb.String()\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error for error wrapping chains.\nfunc (fe *FileError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn fe.Err\n}\n\n// IsFileError checks if an error is a FileError.\nfunc IsFileError(err error) bool {\n\tvar fe *FileError\n\treturn err != nil && (errors.As(err, &fe) || isYAMLErrorOfType(err, ErrorTypeFile))\n}\n\n// SyntaxError represents YAML syntax errors during parsing.\n//\n// SyntaxError includes indentation errors, malformed YAML, and other\n// syntax-related issues that prevent the YAML from being parsed.\ntype SyntaxError struct {\n\tFilePath  string    // Path to the file with syntax error\n\tLine      int       // Line number where syntax error occurred\n\tColumn    int       // Column number where syntax error occurred\n\tMessage   string    // Description of the syntax error\n\tExpected  string    // What was expected (if known)\n\tFound     string    // What was actually found (if known)\n\tErr       error     // Underlying yaml parser error\n\tErrorCode ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (se *SyntaxError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif se.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn se.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeInvalidSyntax\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (se *SyntaxError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeSyntax\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (se *SyntaxError) Context() string {\n\tctx := fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %s\", se.Expected)\n\tif se.Found != \"\" {\n\t\tctx += fmt.Sprintf(\", found: %s\", se.Found)\n\t}\n\treturn ctx\n}\n\n// Error implements the error interface.\nfunc (se *SyntaxError) Error() string {\n\tif se.Line > 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"syntax error in %s at line %d, column %d: %s\", se.FilePath, se.Line, se.Column, se.Message)\n\t}\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"syntax error in %s: %s\", se.FilePath, se.Message)\n}\n\n// Unwrap returns the underlying error.\nfunc (se *SyntaxError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn se.Err\n}\n\n// StructureError represents YAML structure errors during parsing.\n//\n// StructureError includes issues like duplicate keys, invalid nesting,\n// and other structural problems that syntax-checking alone won't catch.\ntype StructureError struct {\n\tFilePath     string    // Path to the file with structure error\n\tLine         int       // Line number where structure error occurred\n\tMessage      string    // Description of the structure error\n\tDuplicateKey string    // Name of duplicate key (if applicable)\n\tLocation     string    // Nested path to the error location\n\tErr          error     // Underlying error\n\tErrorCode    ErrorCode // Error code for programmatic handling\n}\n\n// Code implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ste *StructureError) Code() ErrorCode {\n\tif ste.ErrorCode != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ste.ErrorCode\n\t}\n\tif ste.DuplicateKey != \"\" {\n\t\treturn ErrCodeDuplicateKey\n\t}\n\treturn ErrCodeInvalidStructure\n}\n\n// YAMLErrorType implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ste *StructureError) YAMLErrorType() ErrorType {\n\treturn ErrorTypeStructure\n}\n\n// Context implements YAMLError interface.\nfunc (ste *StructureError) Context() string {\n\tctx := \"location: \" + ste.Location\n\tif ste.DuplicateKey != \"\" {\n\t\tctx += the underlying error.\nfunc (ste *StructureError) Unwrap() error {\n\treturn ste.Err\n}\n\n// TypeMismatchError represents type conversion errors during parsing.\n//\n// TypeMismatchError occurs when
